title: Section 8 Company Registration

hero.headline: Incorporate Your Section 8 Company with Professionals Law
hero.subtext: Establish a legally recognized non-profit and unlock tax benefits, funding opportunities, and social impact potential.

introduction.heading: Introduction and Its Compliance
body: A Section 8 Company is a non-profit organization registered under the Companies Act, 2013. It is formed with the objective to promote commerce, art, science, education, sports, charity, social welfare, religion, environmental protection or any other objective of public welfare.
Unlike regular companies, Section 8 Companies cannot distribute profits to its members and must reinvest earnings into the company's objectives.

Compliance for Section 8 Companies includes annual return filing, maintenance of books of accounts, audits, Board meetings and adherence to Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA) regulations. They are also eligible for tax exemptions and FCRA registration (for foreign donations), subject to specific compliance requirements.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: Registering a Section 8 Company gives your non-profit a formal legal status, making it easier to raise funds, apply for grants, receive donations and establish credibility with donors and government agencies.

It is ideal for individuals or groups aiming to create social impact with legal protection and structured governance. The structure offers strong regulatory backing, which helps in long-term sustainability and accountability.

body.title: Legal Recognition
body.description: A Section 8 Company has a recognized legal identity under Indian law, allowing it to own assets, enter into contracts and operate with legitimacy.

body.title: Tax Exemptions
body.description: The company can apply for Section 12A and 80G registration under the Income Tax Act, providing tax exemptions for the entity and its donors.

body.title: Credibility & Trustworthiness
body.description: Due to strict regulatory oversight, Section 8 Companies gain higher trust from donors, investors, government agencies and CSR partners.

body.title: Separate Legal Entity
body.description: The company’s legal identity is distinct from its members, meaning liabilities and legal matters are confined to the company itself.

body.title: Perpetual Succession
body.description: The entity continues to exist despite changes in directors or members, ensuring long-term continuity and mission fulfillment.

body.title: Access to Foreign Donations
body.description: It can apply for FCRA registration to legally receive foreign contributions, opening up global funding avenues.

body.title: Limited Liability
body.description: Members’ personal assets are protected; their liability is limited to their shareholding or guarantee amount.

criteria: At least 2 directors (for a private company) or 3 directors (for a public company)
Minimum 2 shareholders (can be the same as the directors)
At least one director must be an Indian resident
The entity must be formed for a charitable or not-for-profit objective
Profits must be reinvested in the mission and not distributed to members

documents: Aadhaar Card / Passport / Voter ID / Driving License
Address proof (utility bill, bank statement – not older than 2 months)
Electricity bill / Property tax receipt / Water bill
Declaration of Objectives (explaining the charitable purpose)

body.description: Obtain Digital Signature (DSC) for all proposed directors
Apply for Name Approval through MCA (using RUN form)
Prepare MOA & AOA aligned with charitable objectives
File SPICe+ Form (INC-32) with MCA, along with necessary attachments
Apply for Section 8 License (Form INC-12) with supporting declarations
Receive Incorporation Certificate with a unique CIN and license number
Apply for PAN, TAN and optionally 12A & 80G registration

body.question: Can a Section 8 Company make profits?
body.answer: Yes, but profits must be reinvested in the organization’s mission. Distribution to members is strictly prohibited.

body.question: Is a Section 8 Company better than a Trust or Society?
body.answer: Yes, in terms of structure, credibility, governance and eligibility for international and corporate donations.

body.question: Can foreigners be directors or members?
body.answer: Yes, but at least one director must be an Indian resident.

body.question: Can Section 8 Companies receive foreign donations?
body.answer: Yes, after obtaining FCRA registration from the Ministry of Home Affairs.

body.question: What is the minimum capital required?
body.answer: There is no minimum capital requirement to register a Section 8 Company.

body.question: Can a Section 8 Company convert into a regular company?
body.answer: Yes, but only with approval from the Central Government and subject to regulatory compliance.

body.question: Are audits mandatory for Section 8 Companies?
body.answer: Yes, statutory audits are mandatory regardless of income level.

body.question: Can a Section 8 Company issue shares?
body.answer: No, Section 8 Companies operate on a guarantee basis and cannot issue shares.

body.question: What is the validity of the Section 8 license?
body.answer: It is valid for the lifetime of the company unless revoked due to non-compliance.
